gaps.title: End-to-End Domain Data Pipeline (Raw Docs to Training Data)
gaps.description: Most platforms expect pre-formatted JSONL/CSV datasets. No platform provides a fully automated pipeline that ingests raw domain documents (PDFs, contracts, medical records, code repos), extracts knowledge, and generates high-quality instruction-following training pairs automatically.
gaps.current_coverage: Only Unsloth Studio has Data Recipes for basic doc-to-dataset conversion. Cloud providers have basic data tools but not domain-aware curation.
gaps.affected_users: Enterprise teams with proprietary domain documents but no ML data engineering team

gaps.title: Synthetic Domain Data Generation
gaps.description: Only 2 of 20 platforms (Vertex AI, NeMo) offer any synthetic data generation. There is no platform that uses a large teacher model to automatically generate domain-specific synthetic training data from a knowledge base, taxonomy, or ontology.
gaps.current_coverage: Almost nonexistent in managed platforms. Researchers use ad-hoc scripts with GPT-4/Claude to generate data.
gaps.affected_users: Teams in data-scarce domains (niche industries, low-resource languages, specialized sciences)

gaps.title: Domain-Specific Evaluation Framework
gaps.description: Most platforms offer only generic metrics (loss, perplexity). No platform provides built-in domain-specific evaluation suites for healthcare (clinical accuracy, ICD code matching), legal (citation accuracy, clause extraction), finance (numeric precision, regulatory compliance), etc.
gaps.current_coverage: Databricks has LLM-as-Judge. Most others have zero domain-aware evaluation.
gaps.affected_users: Enterprise teams in regulated industries needing to prove model quality

gaps.title: Continuous Learning / Production Feedback Loop
gaps.description: No platform provides an automated flywheel: capture production failures -> curate corrective examples -> retrain -> deploy -> monitor. Current tools require manual intervention at every step.
gaps.current_coverage: OpenPipe captures production logs. Amdocs built a custom flywheel with NVIDIA Blueprint. No turnkey solution exists.
gaps.affected_users: Production ML teams wanting models that improve over time

gaps.title: Edge/On-Device Fine-Tuning and Deployment Platform
gaps.description: Memory bandwidth (not compute) is the bottleneck for edge LLMs. No unified platform handles the full cycle: fine-tune SLM in cloud -> quantize -> deploy to edge -> collect on-device feedback -> re-fine-tune. On-device personalization remains a research concept.
gaps.current_coverage: Unsloth exports to GGUF/Ollama. SageMaker has edge export. No platform covers the full edge lifecycle.
gaps.affected_users: IoT, mobile, manufacturing, healthcare device teams deploying SLMs at edge

gaps.title: Domain-Adaptive Continued Pretraining as a Service
gaps.description: Continued pretraining on domain corpus is the most effective way to deeply adapt a model, but only 7/20 platforms support it and none make it easy. No platform offers a managed service that takes a domain corpus and automatically runs continued pretraining with the right hyperparameters.
gaps.current_coverage: Axolotl, LLaMA-Factory support it via config. Cloud providers offer it as complex manual setup.
gaps.affected_users: Teams needing deep domain knowledge injection (scientific, legal, medical domains)

gaps.title: Multi-Tenant Domain Model Management
gaps.description: Enterprises serving multiple clients/departments need to manage dozens of domain-specific LoRA adapters with access control, versioning, A/B testing, and cost attribution per tenant. Only Predibase (LoRAX) addresses multi-adapter serving.
gaps.current_coverage: Predibase LoRAX serves 1000s of adapters. No platform provides the full management layer (versioning, access control, cost attribution).
gaps.affected_users: SaaS companies, consulting firms, and enterprises with multiple domain models

gaps.title: Compliance-Aware Training Pipeline for Regulated Industries
gaps.description: Healthcare (HIPAA), finance (SOX, Basel), and legal domains require audit trails, data lineage, model provenance, and compliance certification throughout the fine-tuning pipeline. No platform offers built-in compliance workflows.
gaps.current_coverage: Databricks has governance built in. Lamini and Fireworks have certifications. But no platform has compliance-as-a-feature with automated audit trails for the training pipeline.
gaps.affected_users: Healthcare, financial services, legal, and government teams

gaps.title: Catastrophic Forgetting Prevention and Knowledge Retention Testing
gaps.description: Fine-tuning on domain data often degrades general capabilities. No platform offers automated testing for catastrophic forgetting or techniques to maintain general knowledge while adding domain expertise.
gaps.current_coverage: Academic research on EWC, progressive nets, etc. No platform implements this as a feature.
gaps.affected_users: Any team fine-tuning models for domain tasks that also need general capabilities

gaps.title: Domain Ontology and Terminology Integration
gaps.description: No platform allows importing a domain ontology, taxonomy, or controlled vocabulary to guide tokenization, training data generation, and evaluation. Domain terminology handling is entirely manual.
gaps.current_coverage: Zero coverage. All platforms treat domain knowledge as opaque training data.
gaps.affected_users: Medical, legal, scientific, and engineering domains with formal ontologies

gaps.title: Cost-Optimized Training Advisor
gaps.description: Most teams waste significant compute by choosing wrong base models, wrong training methods, or wrong hyperparameters for their domain task. No platform offers an intelligent advisor that recommends: base model, training method (LoRA vs full vs CPT), data volume needed, and estimated cost.
gaps.current_coverage: Predibase Fine-tuning Index benchmarks models. MonsterAPI auto-optimizes batch size. No holistic advisor exists.
gaps.affected_users: Budget-constrained teams, startups, and non-ML-expert developers

gaps.title: Collaborative Domain Expert Annotation and RLHF Platform
gaps.description: Domain fine-tuning requires domain experts (doctors, lawyers, engineers) to validate outputs and provide preference feedback. No SLM training platform integrates a collaborative annotation/RLHF interface designed for non-technical domain experts.
gaps.current_coverage: Labelbox and Kili Technology offer annotation but are separate from training. No integrated solution within fine-tuning platforms.
gaps.affected_users: Teams collaborating with subject matter experts for RLHF/DPO
